name: Saba (The Unspoiled Queen)
summary: Saba (popularly 'The Unspoiled Queen' in English-language framework) is a Caribbean Leeward Islands civil-law Dutch special municipality (bijzondere gemeente) within the Caribbean Netherlands (BES Islands: Bonaire + Sint Eustatius + Saba) — structurally distinctive globally as the smallest formally-administered Dutch sub-national jurisdiction by area (~13 km² — smallest special-municipality framework in the Kingdom of the Netherlands, smaller than rest-of-Dutch-Caribbean special-municipalities Bonaire ~294 km² + Sint Eustatius ~21 km²), as the only Dutch sub-national jurisdiction operating on an extinct stratovolcano (Mount Scenery 877m — highest point in the entire Kingdom of the Netherlands including European Netherlands, structurally distinctive as the only modern Dutch sub-national jurisdiction with formal volcanic-topographic framework, Saba is essentially a single volcanic island with no flat areas), as one of the most distinctive modern formal-equality LGBT-rights frameworks in the Caribbean (Saba conducted the first same-sex marriage in the entire Caribbean region 9 October 2012 — first legally-recognised same-sex marriage in the Caribbean under Dutch Federal same-sex marriage framework 2001, distinct from rest-of-Caribbean which continued sole-different-sex marriage frameworks), and as a distinctive Atlantic-historical jurisdiction (Saba operated continuous English-language framework in formal-administrative + community-cultural usage despite Dutch sovereignty — distinct from rest-of-Dutch-Caribbean frameworks where Papiamento + Dutch dominate, due to historical English/Scottish/Irish settlement). Saba operated under Dutch West India Company framework 1640s onwards + occasional French + British alternations 17th-19th centuries, operated as part of Netherlands Antilles 1954-2010, and was integrated as Dutch special municipality 10 October 2010 following Netherlands Antilles dissolution framework. Saba comprises ~13 km² with a population of ~1,900. Family-law framework operates under the federal Dutch civil-law framework (Dutch Civil Code 1992 Book 1 Family Law portion) applied uniformly across Dutch sub-national jurisdictions. The Court of First Instance (Saba Branch) is the apex regional court; final appellate jurisdiction lies with the Common Court of Justice of Aruba + Curaçao + Sint Maarten + BES + Supreme Court of the Netherlands. Saba is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label. The Netherlands is a Hague Convention 1980 signatory and party (acceded 12 June 1990 effective 1 September 1990) — Saba is governed by Dutch Hague-Convention-1980 framework via Caribbean Netherlands status.

statutory_framework.citation: Charter for the Kingdom of the Netherlands + 10 October 2010 dissolution framework
statutory_framework.title: Kingdom Charter + Netherlands Antilles dissolution
statutory_framework.relevance: Dutch Federal constitutional framework of 10 October 2010 — Netherlands Antilles dissolution + Saba integration as Dutch special municipality (bijzondere gemeente).

statutory_framework.citation: Dutch Civil Code 1992 Book 1 Family Law portion
statutory_framework.title: Dutch Civil Code Book 1 Family Law
statutory_framework.relevance: Dutch Federal Civil Code 1992 Book 1 Family Law portion applicable uniformly across Saba.

statutory_framework.citation: Dutch Same-Sex Marriage Act 2001 (applicable to BES from 10-10-10)
statutory_framework.title: Dutch Same-Sex Marriage Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Dutch Federal Same-Sex Marriage Act of 21 December 2000 effective 1 April 2001 — first formal same-sex marriage framework globally, applicable to Saba from 10 October 2010.

statutory_framework.title: Hague Convention 1980 — Netherlands
statutory_framework.relevance: Dutch Hague Convention 1980 accession 12 June 1990 effective 1 September 1990 — applicable to Saba via Caribbean Netherlands status.

apex_courts.name: Court of First Instance (Saba Branch)
apex_courts.role: Apex regional trial court for civil and criminal matters from Saba.

apex_courts.name: Common Court of Justice of Aruba, Curaçao, Sint Maarten, and BES
apex_courts.role: Intermediate appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Saba.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of the Netherlands (Hoge Raad)
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Saba.

professional_regulators.name: Dutch Inspectorate for Healthcare and Youth (Inspectie Gezondheidszorg en Jeugd) — Netherlands
professional_regulators.role: Dutch Federal professional regulatory framework applicable in Saba via Caribbean Netherlands status.

anonymisation_convention: Saba family-court decisions are anonymised per Dutch + Caribbean Netherlands court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Dutch West India Company colonisation
key_developments.description: Dutch West India Company colonisation of Saba 1640s — beginning of Dutch colonial framework.

key_developments.title: Netherlands Antilles established
key_developments.description: Netherlands Antilles established 15 December 1954 — Saba incorporated as constituent island.

key_developments.title: Netherlands Antilles dissolution + special municipality integration
key_developments.description: Netherlands Antilles dissolution 10 October 2010 + Saba integration as Dutch special municipality (bijzondere gemeente).

key_developments.title: First same-sex marriage in Caribbean
key_developments.description: First same-sex marriage in the entire Caribbean region conducted in Saba 9 October 2012 — first legally-recognised same-sex marriage in the Caribbean under Dutch Federal same-sex marriage framework 2001.

structural_findings: Saba operates a federal Dutch civil-law special-municipality framework — places Saba in the Caribbean Leeward Islands Dutch special-municipality cluster.
Smallest formally-administered Dutch sub-national jurisdiction by area (~13 km²) is structurally distinctive within Kingdom of the Netherlands cluster — smallest special-municipality framework.

Only Dutch sub-national jurisdiction operating on an extinct stratovolcano (Mount Scenery 877m — highest point in the entire Kingdom of the Netherlands) is structurally distinctive globally — only modern Dutch sub-national jurisdiction with formal volcanic-topographic framework.
One of most distinctive modern formal-equality LGBT-rights frameworks in the Caribbean is structurally distinctive globally — first same-sex marriage in the entire Caribbean region 9 October 2012.

Continuous English-language framework in formal-administrative + community-cultural usage despite Dutch sovereignty is structurally distinctive within Dutch Caribbean cluster — distinct from rest-of-Dutch-Caribbean Papiamento + Dutch frameworks.
2010 Netherlands Antilles dissolution + 10-10-10 framework is structurally distinctive within Dutch Caribbean cluster.
Historical English/Scottish/Irish settlement framework is structurally distinctive within Dutch Caribbean cluster.
Dutch Hague-Convention-1980-accession-1990 framework applies via Caribbean Netherlands status.
editorial_notes: Saba jurisdiction sidecar — civil-law Caribbean Leeward Islands Dutch special-municipality (Charter for the Kingdom of the Netherlands + 10-10-10 framework + Dutch Civil Code 1992 + Dutch Same-Sex Marriage Act 2001 applicable to Saba from 10-10-10 + Mount Scenery 877m + Netherlands Antilles 1954-2010 + 2012 first Caribbean same-sex marriage + Dutch Hague-1980 via Caribbean Netherlands status). Smallest formally-administered Dutch sub-national jurisdiction by area + only Dutch sub-national jurisdiction operating on an extinct stratovolcano globally + first same-sex marriage in the entire Caribbean region 9 October 2012 + continuous English-language framework despite Dutch sovereignty + 10-10-10 Netherlands Antilles dissolution framework.
PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator.

Joins Caribbean-Leeward-Islands + civil-law + Dutch-special-municipality cluster + smallest-Dutch-sub-national-jurisdiction-by-area-globally-distinctive + Mount-Scenery-extinct-stratovolcano-highest-point-Kingdom + first-Caribbean-same-sex-marriage-2012 + continuous-English-language-framework + 10-10-10-Netherlands-Antilles-dissolution + Caribbean-Netherlands-BES + Dutch-Hague-1980-1990 clusters within the corpus.
